seed_problem: Over T rounds pick sensors (paying per activation); coverage a sensor gives decays each round it stays on and carries over when off. Keep every target's per-round coverage >= threshold at min total cost. Composite: set cover x temporal state; trap: static cover is wasteful, must cycle.
mutation: composite: fuse weighted set cover with a per-sensor charge state machine (emitted pulse == current charge; drains when on, recharges when off) and a per-round coverage constraint, so the objective couples WHICH sensors cover WHICH targets with WHEN to rest them for recharge; the optimum is a non-additive duty-cycling schedule, not a static cover.
title: Glacier Sensor Net: Duty-Cycling Under Charge Decay
strategies: all-on baseline: switch every sensor on every round (always feasible, wasteful) -- the checker's reference B
myopic breadth greedy: each round add the sensor covering the most still-deficient targets until demands are met, cost-blind
cost-aware value/cost greedy + redundancy pruning: pick argmax of (charge * deficit)/cost, then drop the most expensive redundant sensors each round
charge-planning local search: reserve freshly-charged sensors, rotate duty across a covering family so every target is met by few high-charge pulses while the rest recharge
